div.p: ☕ Building reliable distributed systems, streaming data infrastructure, and backend platforms.

Final-year Computer Science student passionate about
Distributed Systems • Backend Engineering • Data Infrastructure
I enjoy designing systems that continue to work under failure.
I turn ideas into production-grade systems.
I build systems that are reliable, scalable and observable. My interests lie in distributed systems, streaming pipelines, backend platforms and automation.

ul.li: 🎓 Exploring: System Design, Cloud, and High Performance Systems
💡 Interests: Distributed Systems, Observability, Databases, DevOps
⚡ Fun Fact: I drink coffee and write code. Repeat.

div.p: Message brokers, ordering, consensus, concurrency, persistence, fault tolerance
Kafka, Spark, Airflow, ETL/ELT, real-time ingestion, data pipelines
APIs, microservices, storage, caching, auth, rate limiting
Retries, DLQ, chaos testing, load testing, monitoring, alerts, observability
Real-time market data ingestion using Kafka, Spark Structured Streaming & Airflow with schema validation, DLQ and sub-200ms latency.
High-performance message broker in Go with per-key ordering, persistence, consumer groups, acks & exactly-once semantics (simulated).
Reliable notification delivery with retries, scheduling, priority queues, idempotency keys and Dead Letter Queue.
Real-time endpoint monitoring with Prometheus, Grafana, FastAPI and React dashboard for metrics, alerts and failure analytics.
I'm always open to discussing new ideas, opportunities, or collaborations.

div.div: “ First, solve the problem. Then, write the code. ” — John Johnson

div.p: When you have to explain it, it's bad.
